perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to return to the Company upon reasonable written request, such return to occur within a 30-day period, all Confidential Information that is reasonably accessible, including but not limited to readily available computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original requires immediate return. The modified version allows 30 days and limits the return to reasonably accessible information. This directly contradicts the original's demand for immediacy and completeness, particularly because immediate action and full cooperation are implied throughout the rest of the document, which is an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that only information clearly marked as 'Confidential' disclosed by the Company to Recipient, relating to the Company’s products and explicitly designated business plans, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. Recipient expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Confidential Information, to the extent it is properly documented and legally protected, remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Informati on is granted hereby. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. Recipient agrees to return to the Company upon reasonable written request, such return to occur within a 30-day period, all Confidential Information that is reasonably accessible, including but not limited to readily available computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. Recipient hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have. This Agreem ent shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of California as they apply to contracts entered into and wholly to be performed in the State of California. The federal and state courts within the State of Calif ornia shall have exclusive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Prior to taking YOUR exam, you will be required to accept the terms and conditions of the VMware NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA). You will view the agreement on screen before the exam starts. If you choose NOT to agree to the NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) after you select Begin Exam, your exam will be immediately cancelled and you will forfeit your e xam fee. BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND C ONDITIONS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE EXITED FROM THE EXAM.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires immediate return of all Confidential Information. The changed text modifies this to a 30-day return period for only 'reasonably accessible' information. The contradicted text (which contains the changed text) refers to this new 30-day return period and 'reasonably accessible' limitation. However, the explanation states that the immediate return is implied throughout the rest of the document, therefore there is a direct contradiction, making it a "YES" because of the change in immediacy and scope of what needs to be returned.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information could cause irreparable harm an d significant injury which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that the Company shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies it may have.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Company believes that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information *might* cause harm and injury. Accordingly, the Company *may* seek injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This weakens the consequence of disclosure. 'Could cause irreparable harm' becomes 'might cause harm', and 'shall have the right' becomes 'may seek'. This contradicts the severity implied by the rest of the NDA which suggests a strong stance, especially given the original wording indicates they could seek and obtain immediate injunction which is an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that only information clearly marked as 'Confidential' disclosed by the Company to Recipient, relating to the Company’s products and explicitly designated business plans, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. Recipient agrees to return to the Company upon reasonable written request, such return to occur within a 30-day period, all Confidential Information that is reasonably accessible, including but not limited to readily available computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. Recipient acknowledges that the Company believes that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information *might* cause harm and injury. This Agreem ent shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of California as they apply to contracts entered into and wholly to be performed in the State of California. If you choose NOT to agree to the NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) after you select Begin Exam, your exam will be immediately cancelled and you will forfeit your e xam fee. BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND C ONDITIONS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE EXITED FROM THE EXAM.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'could cause irreparable harm' to 'might cause harm' and 'shall have the right' to 'may seek' weakens the protective stance initially implied throughout the NDA. While the contradicted text contains the changed sentence, its presence now introduces a contradiction within the document, as the weakened language conflicts with the expectation of strong protection of confidential information elsewhere, creating an Ambiguity-In Text Contradiction. The original text implies a stronger legal stance, while the changed text makes the Company's recourse less certain, and more lenient.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreem ent shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of California as they apply to contracts entered into and wholly to be performed in the State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, but enforcement may be subject to the laws of the jurisdiction where the Recipient resides.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, enforcement was firmly under California law. Now, it introduces potential conflicts of law, dependent on the recipient's location. This change creates significant ambiguity, as it is now unclear which laws truly dictate enforcement, which may be contradictory depending on the Recipient's location.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that only information clearly marked as 'Confidential' disclosed by the Company to Recipient, relating to the Company’s products and explicitly designated business plans, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient. Recipient expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, but enforcement may be subject to the laws of the jurisdiction where the Recipient resides. Prior to taking YOUR exam, you will be required to accept the terms and conditions of the VMware NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA). You will view the agreement on screen before the exam starts. If you choose NOT to agree to the NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) after you select Begin Exam, your exam will be immediately cancelled and you will forfeit your e xam fee. BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND C ONDITIONS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE EXITED FROM THE EXAM.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is governed and enforced by California law. The changed text modifies this by stating that while the agreement is governed by California law, enforcement may be subject to the laws of the jurisdiction where the recipient resides. This introduces ambiguity and potential conflict, as enforcement may no longer be solely under California law, directly contradicting the initial statement in the contradicted text, which applies California law throughout the agreement. The original text in the contradicted section expects that all enforcement will be under California Law, but the changed section breaks this expectation.

perturbation.original_text: BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND C ONDITIONS.
perturbation.changed_text: BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU ACKNOWLEDGE THAT YOU HAVE HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO REVIEW THIS AGREEMENT AND INTEND TO BE GENERALLY FAMILIAR WITH ITS KEY POINTS.
perturbation.explanation: This weakens the binding nature of the agreement. Instead of stating the user *agrees* to be bound, it states they have *had the opportunity to review*. This conflicts with the NDA's overall purpose as it indicates a formal agreement, which assumes the Recipient is aware of the commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Prior to taking YOUR exam, you will be required to accept the terms and conditions of the VMware NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA). You will view the agreement on screen before the exam starts. If you choose NOT to agree to the NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) after you select Begin Exam, your exam will be immediately cancelled and you will forfeit your e xam fee. BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU ACKNOWLEDGE THAT YOU HAVE HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO REVIEW THIS AGREEMENT AND INTEND TO BE GENERALLY FAMILIAR WITH ITS KEY POINTS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE EXITED FROM THE EXAM.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the user *agrees* to be bound by the terms, indicating a definitive commitment. The changed text weakens this to acknowledging an *opportunity to review* and intending to be *generally familiar*. This creates an ambiguity and directly conflicts with the contradicted text because it no longer clearly establishes that the user is legally bound by the agreement. This contradiction fits the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as the binding nature of the agreement is inconsistently defined. The contradicted section requires agreement to the terms, whereas the changed section only requires a general familiarity.
